steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Rhetorical Synthesis question. The goal is to identify "the sculpture for which Arp won a Carnegie Prize gold medal" — the answer must name the specific artwork (Sculpture classique) and link it to the prize win.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'In 1964, an artist from Germany won a Carnegie Prize gold medal for a sculpture.': Mentions a sculpture but never names it; also omits Arp's name. Does not identify the specific sculpture. ✗
  • Choice B — 'The Carnegie Museum of Art awarded Arp a gold medal in 1964.': Names Arp and the medal but never names the sculpture at all. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Arp won a Carnegie Prize gold medal for the sculpture Sculpture classique.': Names Arp, names the sculpture Sculpture classique, and connects them to the prize win — directly accomplishing the goal. ✓
  • Choice D — 'The Carnegie Prize is an international art competition administered by the Carnegie Museum of Art in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania.': Describes the prize but mentions neither Arp nor the sculpture. Irrelevant to the goal. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
